arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Club Brugge - Wed, Mar 12th 2025, 20:00 Aston Villa supporters enjoyed yet another spectacular pre-match show ahead of our UEFA Champions League round of 16 second leg against Club Brugge. Villa Park was transformed into an awe-inspiring spectacle showcasing history, pride and ambition for our latest European night under the lights in B6. Inspired by the Gas Lamp under which our club was ignited, the breathtaking illuminated tifo display symbolised the club’s enduring light and embraced the flame that set Villa’s journey into motion, has illuminated our past triumphs and guides us towards future glory. The tifo featured the captains who have lifted our most prestigious trophies, embodying our history and ambition. European Cup-winning captain Dennis Mortimer was celebrated alongside League Championship (First Division)-winning leaders John Devey (1894, 1896 & 1897), Jimmy Crabtree (1899 & 1900), Joe Bache (1910) and Mortimer (1981). FA Cup-winning skippers Archie Hunter (1887), Devey (1895 & 1897), Howard Spencer (1905), Bache (1913), Andy Ducat (1920) and Johnny Dixon (1957) were also honoured. As were League Cup winners Vic Crowe (1961), Ian Ross (1975), Chris Nicholl (1977), Kevin Richardson (1994) and Andy Townsend (1996). Villa Park was a sea of claret and blue as every supporter received a commemorative Champions League campaign scarf - a symbol of their place in the shared moment: one Villa, one family, one dream. Using specially installed search lights and pitch lights, the stadium also created an immersive experience that captured the essence of light as our guiding force alongside the glow of the Gas Lamp in a dazzling display. The dramatic light show was cut to high-tempo classical music, blending tradition with the pulse of modern football to underscore footage of Villa’s greatest moments - our captains lifting silverware, the roar of past European nights and the unwavering support of our fans. It all combined to provide a special moment, connecting every supporter in unity and igniting Villa Park in a celebration of our past, present and future. Villa’s journey began under a Gas Lamp, where a flame was lit - a light that has guided generations of heroes. Now, as we once again look to progress on the grandest stage, that same fire burns in every fan, every player, every moment. Prepared. UTV!

arts.ttl: Aston Villa team news vs Club Brugge confirmed as Martinez and Rashford decisions made
arts.sum: Unai Emery has named his Aston Villa side to face Club Brugge in the second leg of their Champions League round of 16 tie Aston Villa manager Unai Emery has named his starting XI to face Club Brugge in the Champions League this evening.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa vs. Club Brugge: Team news confirmed for Champions League game, do Emiliano Martinez, Marco Asensio start?
arts.sum: Aston Villa and Club Brugge confirm their first XIs for the second leg of their Champions League last-16 tie.

arts.ttl: Martínez returns to face Club Brugge
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Club Brugge - Wed, Mar 12th 2025, 20:00 Aston Villa's starting XI has been announced ahead of Wednesday night's UEFA Champions League last 16 tie against Club Brugge. With his side 3-1 up from the first leg in Bruges, manager Unai Emery has opted to make five changes to the team that beat Brentford 1-0 on Saturday. Emi Martínez returns in goal while Ian Maatsen replaces Lucas Digne at left-back and Matty Cash is in for Axel Disasi on the other side. Further forward, Boubacar Kamara comes into midfield and Marcus Rashford joins the attack. Amadou Onana and Marco Asensio return to bolster the subs bench. Here are the teams in full... Aston Villa: Martínez, Cash, Konsa, Mings, Maatsen, Kamara, Tielemans, Rashford, Rogers, McGinn, Watkins. Subs: Olsen, Zych, Bogarde, Disasi, Digne, Torres, Asensio, Onana, Ramsey, Bailey.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa’s Marco Asensio can play against PSG in the Champions League
arts.sum: When Paris Saint-Germain loaned Marco Asensio (29) out to Aston Villa in January, they perhaps didn’t expect to be reunited with the Spaniard quite so soon. However, an unlikely reunion…

arts.ttl: Aston Villa v Club Brugge, Arsenal v PSV: Champions League last 16, second legs – live
arts.sum: Champions League updates, 8pm kick-offs, and more Live scores | Read today’s Football Daily | And mail Will “You have to feel for Nathan Butler-Oyedeji,” writes Kieran McKintosh. “He’s 22, still not had a proper first-team appearance, not even scored on loan at Cheltenham or Accrington, and even in a game like this where it basically doesn’t matter who Arteta plays, a defender who is leaving at the end of the season gets a start up top before he does. Ouch.” I have a feeling the young lad’s long-term future is not at Arsenal. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Aston Villa eyeing summer move for Real Sociedad star
arts.sum: Aston Villa have regularly looked to the La Liga market in the last couple of years, and they could do so again to address the right-back position in Unai Emery’s squad. Despite having signed Andres Garcia from Levante during the winter transfer window, it appears that they have plans to make another signing in the […] The post Aston Villa eyeing summer move for Real Sociedad star appeared first on Football España.

arts.ttl: Man Utd transfer news: Marcus Rashford to embarrass Red Devils one last time
arts.sum: Manchester United ’s Marcus Rashford woes are not over despite successfully loaning the player to Aston Villa at the end of the transfer window. United to bank just €24m from Rashford sale

arts.ttl: Aston Villa vs Club Brugge LIVE match updates and team news from Champions League clash
arts.sum: Villa last hosted a Belgian side in any European competition back in April 1982 in the European Cup semi-final against Anderlecht, winning 1-0 with a goal from Tony Morley on their way to winning the trophy. Unai Emery's host the Belgian champions with a two-goal lead after winning 3-1 at the Jan Breydel Stadium last Tuesday.
